assertions.expectation: Proposes a phased approach: control plane first, then CPU pools, then T4 dev, then inference (A100), then training (H100) last
assertions.reason: Response clearly outlines 4 phases in correct order: CPU Services (Day 1), T4 Development (Day 2), A100 Inference (Day 3-4), H100 Training (Day 5-7), with control plane upgrade shown first in Phase 1

assertions.expectation: Control plane can be upgraded independently without affecting running workloads
assertions.reason: Response explicitly shows control plane upgrade first with command 'gcloud container clusters upgrade ml-platform --master' before node pool upgrades

assertions.expectation: CPU service pools upgraded first as lowest risk — use standard surge settings (maxSurge=2-3)
assertions.reason: Response uses maxSurge=20 for CPU pools, which is much higher than the expected standard surge settings of 2-3

assertions.expectation: T4 dev pool upgraded next — dev workloads are tolerant of interruption
assertions.reason: Response correctly places T4 dev pool as Phase 2 and notes 'can pause dev work during upgrade window' showing understanding of interruption tolerance

assertions.expectation: A100 inference pool upgraded with careful surge settings (maxSurge=1) and PDBs to maintain serving capacity
assertions.reason: Response mentions PDBs in risk mitigation section but doesn't specify maxSurge=1 for A100 pools; instead uses blue-green strategy or maxSurge=0/maxUnavailable=10

assertions.expectation: H100 training pool upgraded last, during a scheduled gap between training runs — with cordon-and-wait or maxSurge=0/maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Response places H100 as Phase 4 (last), mentions coordinating with training schedule gaps, and shows cordon-and-wait approach with maxSurge=0/maxUnavailable settings

assertions.expectation: Notes the 20-node parallelism limit — at 5,000 nodes, the full upgrade will take weeks and must span multiple maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response mentions 20 parallel max for A100 pool but doesn't explicitly discuss the 20-node parallelism limit as a general GKE constraint or explain that 5,000 nodes will take weeks due to this limit

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver compatibility testing in staging before each GPU pool upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes GPU-specific considerations section with driver compatibility validation commands and notes about testing PyTorch/JAX/TensorFlow compatibility in dev environment first

assertions.expectation: Addresses GPUDirect/RDMA networking validation after upgrading the H100 pool
assertions.reason: Response includes specific section on RDMA/High-Performance Networking with commands to verify MTU and networking config, and compact placement preservation for GPUDirect-TCPX
